symptomSummary: Prompt evolution initially improves based on feedback but degrades after ~30 invocations, addressing old problems instead of recent issues.
rootCauseHypothesis: When fetching feedback for evolution, the system likely applies a limit (around 30) without properly sorting by recency or weighting recent feedback higher, causing old feedback to dominate the evolution context.
fixHypothesis: Add recency-based sorting (ORDER BY timestamp DESC) to feedback queries and/or implement time-decay weighting so recent thumbs-down signals override older accumulated feedback.

primaryLocation.rationale: This service orchestrates prompt evolution and likely contains the logic for fetching and preparing feedback data. The threshold behavior (~30 invocations) suggests a query limit without proper recency sorting happening at this orchestration layer.

candidateLocations.rationale: Recently modified repository layer that likely contains the actual database queries for fetching invocation/feedback data. A LIMIT clause without ORDER BY timestamp DESC would cause exactly this symptom.
This agent likely constructs the evolution prompt from feedback. If feedback weighting or filtering happens here rather than at the query layer, recent signals might not be prioritized.
API endpoint that triggers evolution. May contain parameters or limits that affect which feedback is considered, though less likely to be the root cause.
